title: A Strange Wild Song
content:
He thought he saw an Elephant
That practiced on a fife:
He looked again, and found it was
A letter from his wife.
ï¿½At length I realize,ï¿½ he said,
ï¿½The bitterness of life!ï¿½

He thought he saw a Buffalo
Upon the chimney-piece:
He looked again, and found it was
His Sisterï¿½s Husbandï¿½s Niece.
ï¿½Unless you leave this house,ï¿½ he said,
ï¿½Iï¿½ll send for the police!ï¿½

he thought he saw a Rattlesnake
That questioned him in Greek:
He looked again, and found it was
The Middle of Next Week.
ï¿½The one thing I regret,ï¿½ he said,
ï¿½Is that it cannot speak!ï¿½

He thought he saw a Bankerï¿½s Clerk
Descending from the bus:
He looked again, and found it was
A Hippopotamus.
ï¿½If this should stay to dine,ï¿½ he said,
ï¿½There wonï¿½t be much for us!ï¿½

He thought he saw a Kangaroo
That worked a Coffee-mill:
He looked again, and found it was
A Vegetable-Pill.
ï¿½Were I to swallow this,ï¿½ he said,
ï¿½I should be very ill!ï¿½

He thought he saw a Coach-and-Four
That stood beside his bed:
He looked again, and found it was
A Bear without a Head.
ï¿½Poor thing,ï¿½ he said, ï¿½poor silly thing!
Itï¿½s waiting to be fed!ï¿½
meaning:
This evocative piece by Lewis Carroll, titled "A Strange Wild Song", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
